posture: Inform — a clean scan: no fresh, dated NSW-REZ development cleared the relevance bar in the 24 hours to Monday morning, and the weekend pick-up (Saturday into Monday) added nothing new. The free source register produced no new agency release, market notice, planning step or developer disclosure with a REZ nexus: EnergyCo's latest-updates feed showed nothing published after the window opened, the AEMO market-notices feed carried no new REZ-region constraint or connection event, and the New England network-operator, corridor-refinement and Central-West Orana pages held no new dated material. Every live thread the radar carries moved earlier and is deduplicated out as a prior-digest carry-forward: Squadron Energy's decision not to proceed with the 426MW Boorolong wind project inside the New England REZ (16 July) and the political fight it fed, with the state government branding it the 'first investment victim' of the Coalition's late-June pledge to scrap the zone and its 500kV Bayswater-Walcha line and Squadron rejecting any policy motive; the New England Draft REZ Access Scheme Declaration still on public exhibition (feedback by 6 August, declaration targeted end-September) after EnergyCo's 14 July webinars, with the EIS expected to be lodged and exhibited in the second half of 2026 and energisation targeted for 2032; the refined 1km Muswellbrook-Walcha corridor and its July-September field-investigation program across roughly 150 affected landholders, against live Walcha High Country Guardians opposition and the 22,000-signature petition; EnergyCo's 15 July South West REZ Project Development Deed with Transgrid (Jerilderie-Wagga Wagga, unlocking 3.56GW for the four access-rights projects) and Squadron's 14 July Banandra 330kV bypass; the six-developer 'Inland Renewable Energy Region' push into AEMO's Draft 2026 ISP for a far/north-west NSW zone (a reboot of the earlier far-west proposal, low direct nexus to the five declared REZs); and on Central-West Orana the Port-to-REZ OSOM delivery campaign to Squadron's Uungula Wind Farm, the Main Road No. 709 gazettal, and ACEREZ's staged access-track recommencement under stronger heritage controls with the DPHI rock-shelter assessment still open, plus the community roadshow flagged for 11-15 August. Source gaps to log rather than read as absence of news: ABC News and The Guardian Australia surfaced no new dated REZ item in this run, and the paid mastheads (AFR, SMH, the ACM regional titles) produced no fresh REZ headline even at standfirst level, so no paywalled items are carried today. Nothing in the scan changes the standing posture: the dominant exposures remain the New England political and social-licence contest ahead of the March 2027 state election, and the delivery and heritage risks on Central-West Orana.
summary: No fresh NSW-REZ-relevant item surfaced in the 24-hour window to Monday 20 July morning — a clean weekend pick-up across the free source register, with no new EnergyCo release, AEMO market notice, planning step or developer disclosure carrying a REZ nexus. All active threads are prior-digest carry-forwards with no movement in the window: the Squadron Boorolong exit and the New England political fight over the Coalition's plan to scrap the 500kV line, the New England Access Scheme exhibition (feedback by 6 August), the July-September corridor field investigations and Walcha opposition, the 15 July South West REZ Transgrid export-upgrade deed, the 14 July Squadron Banandra bypass, the Inland Renewable Energy Region ISP submission, and the Central-West Orana OSOM campaign, Main Road No. 709 gazettal and ACEREZ heritage matter. ABC News, The Guardian Australia and the paid mastheads (AFR, SMH, ACM titles) surfaced no new dated REZ item in this run and are logged as a source gap. The digest is published with empty sections so the archive shows continuity.
